relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Oversight is described as a beautiful work, not a status trophy.
The overseer’s life must be steady, hospitable, teachable, and trustworthy.
Gentleness replaces violence, quarrels, and greed.
Household care becomes evidence of relational maturity.
Care for the assembly is tested by care close to home.
Newly planted pride can become a trap.
A good witness outside protects the work.
Servants/deacons also need dignity, honesty, and freedom from shameful gain.
They hold the mystery of trust in a pure conscience.
Women likewise are named with dignity and trustworthiness.
Servants/deacons are also measured by faithful household care.
Beautiful service produces good standing and bold trust.
These instructions are written while Paul hopes to come soon.
The assembly is household of the living Divine, pillar and support of truth.
The mystery is great: manifested in flesh, vindicated in Spirit, proclaimed, trusted, and taken in glory.

contemplative_note: 1 Timothy 3 treats leadership as tested care. The mystery is not status but embodied reverence, pure conscience, and the living Divine’s household holding truth.
